title: Structure multiplexing-based miniaturized high-isolation common-caliber antenna
abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of common-caliber antennas and particularly provides a structure multiplexing-based miniaturized high-isolation common-caliber antenna used for further reducing the occupied area of the common-caliber antenna. According to the structure multiplexing-based miniaturized high-isolation common-caliber antenna, firstly, a short-circuit point is introduced into the center of a patch antenna by using the characteristic that an electric field of the patch antenna is zero at the center of the patch antenna, so that the occupied area of the patch antenna is reduced on the basis of not changing the operating frequency of the patch antenna, thereby achieving the target of miniaturizing a sub-6G patch antenna; secondly, a substrate integrated waveguide antenna is multiplexed with a feed structure as the patch antenna, thereby achieving the common-caliber design of the substrate integrated waveguide antenna and the patch antenna; and finally the miniaturi

title: FPGA-BASED RATE-ADAPTIVE SPATIALLY-COUPLED LDPC CODES FOR OPTICAL COMMUNICATIONS
abstract: Disclosed are systems, methods, and software for generating spatially-coupled low-density parity-check (SC-LDPC) codes. A method for generating SC-LDPC codes includes generating one or more quasi-cyclic low-density parity-check (QC-LDPC) codes, and also includes assigning at least one of the generated one or more QC-LDPC codes as one or more template codes. The method further includes copying at least a portion of the one or more template codes to introduce irregularity. The method also includes shifting one or more template codes on a sub-block basis to generate at least one SC-LDPC code. As compared to known LDPC code generation modalities, the disclosed invention provides a simplified technique for implementation in streamlined hardware which has more general applicability across both present, and anticipated, communication systems, including those adapted for use with optical communications, wireless communications, and 5G as well as future 6G.

title: ANTENNA ASSEMBLY FOR TERMINAL WITH FOLDABLE SCREEN AND TERMINAL
abstract: A terminal with a foldable screen and an antenna assembly thereof are disclosed. The antenna assembly includes a foldable frame and seven antenna modules. The frame includes a first side frame; a second side frame opposite to the first side frame, a third side frame, and a fourth side frame. A first antenna portion is disposed at a corner connecting the first side frame to the third side frame. A second antenna portion is disposed at a corner connecting the second side frame and the third side frame. A third antenna portion is disposed on the second sub-frame. Fourth, fifth, and sixth antenna portions are sequentially arranged on the fourth side frame. A seventh antenna portion is disposed on the fourth sub-frame. At least 2*2 MIMO configuration of WIFI frequency band and 4*4 MIMO configuration of 5G NR frequency band below Sub-6G frequency band are formed by the antenna modules.

title: Device for detecting stability of Sub 6G frequency band of wireless 5G product
abstract: The utility model discloses a wireless 5G product Sub 6G frequency band stability detection device which comprises a mounting plate, through holes are formed in the front end face and the rear end face of the mounting plate, connecting blocks are arranged in the through holes and extend out of the mounting plate, and fixing grooves are formed in the peripheries of the outer surfaces of the connecting blocks. A connecting piece is fixedly mounted on the front end face of the connecting block, a detection device body is fixedly mounted on the front end face of the connecting piece, threaded holes are formed in the two sides of the outer surface of the mounting plate correspondingly, moving grooves are formed in the opposite sides of the two threaded holes correspondingly, and movable groovesare formed in the inner sides of the two moving grooves correspondingly; and a screw rod is arranged on the inner side of the moving groove. According to the utility model, no tools are needed when th

title: Broadband band-pass filter for sixth-generation mobile communication and resonance module of broadband band-pass filter
abstract: The invention relates to a broadband band-pass filter for sixth-generation mobile communication and a resonance module of the broadband band-pass filter. The resonance module comprises four layers offrequency selection surface electromagnetic resonance units which are arranged at equal intervals, each frequency selection surface electromagnetic resonance unit comprises a square block layer, wherein the square block layer is provided with three square frames which are sequentially nested from inside to outside by taking the center of the square block layer as the center, the three square frames are all formed by hollowing out, and every two adjacent square frames are connected at an angle of 45 degrees. Compared with the prior art, the filter is oriented to sixth-generation mobile communication, realizes 0.7THz bandwidth, and has 90% high transmittance.

title: 6G terahertz satellite communication receiving device
abstract: The invention discloses a 6G terahertz satellite communication receiving device, which comprises a mounting base and a rotary connecting base. title: Implementation method of compact multi-layer transmitting-receiving antenna device
abstract: The invention discloses an implementation method of a compact multi-layer transmitting-receiving antenna device, the compact multi-layer transmitting-receiving antenna device implemented by adopting the method has three metal layers, and two dielectric plates are arranged among the three metal layers; the top metal layer is an antenna array layer (100); the middle metal layer is a metal ground layer (110), and a plurality of slotting gaps (51, 52, 53 and 5n) arranged according to a certain set position are formed in one side of the metal ground layer (110); the bottom metal layer (120) is an electromagnetic lens (130); the compact multi-layer transmitting-receiving antenna device realized by adopting the method can form radiation beams in different directions, realizes electronic scanning,and can be used for scenes of 5G and 6G millimeter wave communication, millimeter wave satellite communication, millimeter wave radar and the like; and according to the technical scheme, compared with

title: 6G communication antenna array element position tolerance determination method based on channel capacity sensitivity
abstract: The invention discloses a 6G communication antenna array element position tolerance determination method based on channel capacity sensitivity, and the method comprises the steps: determining that a design index of the array element position tolerance is the electric field intensity of an array antenna, and calculating the electrical performance of the array antenna under an ideal condition; calculating the array element position error sensitivity and describing the increasing speed of the array element tolerance in iteration; generating an array element random normal error sample; calculatingantenna electrical performance change conditions corresponding to all the samples; judging the number of error samples meeting performance requirements; calculating the tolerance of the last iteration and outputting the array element position tolerance for the electric field intensity and the channel capacity of the array antenna; and comparing the position tolerances, taking the smaller positiont

title: ANTENNA MODULE FOR USE IN FOLDING SCREEN TERMINAL AND TERMINAL
assignee: AAC ACOUSTIC TECH SHENZHEN CO LTD [CN]
abstract: Provided in the present application are an antenna module for use in a folding screen terminal and a terminal comprising said antenna module, the antenna module comprising a folding frame and seven antenna modules; the frame comprises a first edge frame comprising fifth and sixth edge frames arranged symmetrically and a second side frame comprising seventh and eighth edge frames arranged symmetrically arranged opposite to one another, and third and fourth side edge frames arranged opposite one another; a first antenna part is arranged at the connecting point of the first edge frame and the third side edge frame; a second antenna part is arranged at the connecting point of the second edge frame and the third side edge frame; a third antenna part is arranged on the sixth edge frame; fourth, fifth, and sixth antenna parts are arranged at intervals on the fourth side edge frame; and a seventh antenna part is arranged on the eighth edge frame; the first, second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth,

title: Horn antenna and electronic equipment
assignee: HEFEI LCFC INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a horn antenna and electronic equipment. The horn antenna comprises a horn body, a first antenna, a plurality of frequency modulation circuits and a switch, the first antenna,the frequency modulation circuits and the switch are arranged on the inner surface of the horn body. Each frequency modulation circuit is electrically connected with the first antenna through the switch; and the switch is further electrically connected with a controller, and the switch is used for receiving a control signal of the controller and conducting a corresponding frequency modulation circuit according to the control signal so as to adjust the working frequency of the first antenna. According to the invention, Sub-6G, MIMO and WLAN antennas are integrated on the horn body, and a change-over switch and a plurality of frequency modulation circuits are added, so that the antenna can be adaptively adjusted at a low frequency, more internal space of a machine is saved, the design of a na

title: User-centric unmanned aerial vehicle base station multi-beam joint transmission method
abstract: An unmanned aerial vehicle base station becomes an important candidate solution for future B5G/6G network coverage and capacity enhancement by means of high mobility, higher line-of-sight probabilityand flexible deployment characteristics. However, the communication capability of a single unmanned aerial vehicle is limited, and durable and stable services cannot be provided for users. Meanwhile,the large-probability line-of-sight link of the unmanned aerial vehicle also brings strong same-frequency interference while improving the quality of a service link. Therefore, in order to ensure theconsistency and continuity of unmanned aerial vehicle network services, the invention provides an unmanned aerial vehicle base station group taking a user as a center, and unmanned aerial vehicles inthe group serve the user at the same time in a joint transmission (JT) mode so as to enhance the robustness of data transmission. title: Spectrally encapsulated orthogonal frequency division multiplexing method
abstract: The out-of-band emission (OoBE) problem of cyclic prefix (CP) orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM), which has been unresolved in 4G techniques, needs to be resolved for efficient frequency resource utilization in 6G mobile communication techniques. This research is for specific implementation of orthogonalization, which is a core technique for spectral encapsulation (SE) resulting in OFDM signal spectrum formation. SE-OFDM uses no long filters, is a spectral shaping technique based on zero insertion, and thus is applicable to a short OFDM symbol. According to the present invention, a precoder is generated with an accurate orthogonal matrix and complete blocking is performed at the frequency bandwidth boundary. The information transmission part of SE-OFDM of the present invention is identical to CP-OFDM. Accordingly, it can be realized using IFFT with the BER performance of CP-OFDM retained. As a result, substantial improvement can be achieved in terms of complexity.

title: Copper alloy material for 6G communication connector and preparation method of copper alloy material
assignee: GRINM ENG TECH RES INST CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a copper alloy material for a 6G communication connector and a preparation method of the copper alloy material, and belongs to the technical field of non-ferrous metal processing. The copper alloy material is composed of 3.4-5.5 wt% of Ni, 0.6-1.5 wt% of Si, 0.6-1.2 wt% of Ti, 0.1-0.3 wt% of Cr, 0.01-1.0 wt% of other elements and the balance Cu. According to the copper alloy material for the 6G communication connector and the preparation method of the copper alloy material, the tensile strength of the copper alloy material is 950-1100 MPa, the elongation is 5-15%, the conductivity is 18-35% IACS, the stress relaxation rate of 150 DEG C*1000 h is 10-20%, no defect occurs when the copper alloy material is bent by 90 degrees in the transverse direction and the longitudinal direction when R/T is equal to 3, and the copper alloy material has good comprehensive performance and can meet the use requirement of the 6G communication connector.

The integrated controller is electrically connected with the three single-lamp controllers, the storage battery, the 5G/6G base station antenna and the power generation device, and the storage battery is electrically connected with the power generation device. title: Back-drilling composite cover plate for controlling drilling depth of circuit board and processing method
abstract: The invention relates to a back-drilling composite cover plate for controlling the drilling depth of a circuit board and a processing method. title: Film material of bridge island type conductive substance
abstract: The invention discovers and proposes for the first time that the conductive layer of the conductive substance film material has the phenomena of ohmic conduction and capacitive conduction, and also has the phenomenon of inductive conduction (inductive reactance). The film material of the bridge island type conductive material is a conductive film for overcoming inductive reactance on the surface of the conductive film, and bridge island type physical contact (sintering) points are constructed for the conductive materials with different shapes, so that the control on the micro current rheological direction is realized, the high-frequency alternating inductive reactance (or reverse utilization) of a conductive ink layer is reduced, and the film material is used for each product process and has outstanding cost performance; and hot melt pressure bonding, electronic printing, compounding, light, heat, electronic curing, and etching may be performed. title: Pillar-shaped semiconductor device and manufacturing method thereof
abstract: In a SRAM cell, a Si pillar 6a, which is a selection SGT in Si pillars 6a to 6e in an upper row, is located on the left-end in X direction. A Si pillar 6j, which is a selection SGT in Si pillars 6f to 6j in a lower row, is located on the right end. The Si pillar 6g of the lower row is present in a width of an area extended from a contact hole 47a in Y direction in planar view. Then, the Si pillar 6d of the upper row is present in a width of an area extended from a contact hole 47b in Y direction in planar view. In the upper row, a TiN layer 40b, which is a gate electrode for a loading SGT and a driving SGT, is formed to contact at side surface of entire gate region in a vertical direction between the Si pillars 6c, 6d, and 6e. Similarly, in the lower row, a TiN layer 40c, which is a gate electrode for the loading SGT and the driving SGT, is formed to contact at side surface of entire gate region in a vertical direction between the Si pillars 6f, 6g, and 6h.

title: Novel material layer structure of high-frequency circuit board
abstract: The utility model discloses a novel material layer structure of a high-frequency circuit board. The novel material layer structure comprises a cured PI film, an upper semi-cured TFP film coated on thefront surface of the cured PI film, and an upper copper foil layer laminated on the upper semi-cured TFP film. The novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board provided by the utility model has high-frequency characteristics, has the performance of high-speed transmission of high-frequency signals, can adapt to the current high-frequency high-speed trend from a wireless network to terminal application, and is especially suitable for novel 5G and 6G scientific and technological products; as an integral structure, the novel material layer structure of the high-frequency circuit board can be used as a circuit board manufacturing material in a subsequent circuit board manufacturing process to manufacture circuit board structures such as a single-layer circuit board, a mul

title: Power amplifier for sub-6G frequency band
assignee: HANGZHOU LINGXIN MICRO ELECTRONICS CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a power amplifier for a sub 6G frequency band, and the power amplifier comprises a complementary power amplifier circuit, of which the bias voltage is provided by a reference voltage circuit; the starting circuit, the positive temperature coefficient reference circuit, the negative temperature coefficient reference circuit and the bias voltage circuit are connected with thepositive temperature coefficient reference circuit and the negative temperature coefficient reference circuit respectively, the first bias current and the second bias current are set according to a certain proportion, and finally reference voltage is generated; the linearity index of the power amplifier is improved and stabilized through a complementary structure and temperature compensation.

The invention provides aradio frequency switch for a sub 6G frequency band, the radio frequency switch adopts a two-stage structure, and the radio frequency switch not only has a wider frequency band range, but also can effectively reduce the parasitic capacitance from a radio frequency antenna to the ground, improves the isolation degree between ports, effectively improves the linearity of a circuit and reduces the insertion loss. title: ELECTRONIC CIRCUIT
abstract: An electronic circuit (4) comprises at least one electrically conductive portion arranged to a substrate (2) and at least one electrical coupling point (5) determined at the at least one electrically conductive portion. title: SaaS-based identity recognition and payment method in remote monitoring system
abstract: The invention provides an SaaS (Software as a Service)-based identity recognition and payment method in a remote monitoring system, which comprises the following steps of: acquiring user sign information data, user identity information and user expense payment authorization information at a terminal, performing user identity information recognition and user expense payment authorization verification at a management platform, and paying the expense, wherein the management platform communicates with the terminal through a 5G or 6G wireless network. According to the SaaS-based identity recognition and payment method in the remote monitoring system, the collected user physical sign information is prevented from being confused with another due to human reasons or accidental reasons, and remotemonitoring service interruption due to the fact that service fees are not paid in time is avoided.

title: 6G RIS-assisted physical layer security joint optimization method in NOMA system
abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of wireless communication, and discloses a 6G RIS-assisted physical layer security joint optimization method in a NOMA system. The method comprises the steps: in a downlink MISO communication network with one base station BS and two users U1 (a near-end user) and U2 (a far-end user), for the two users are located in a communication dead zone of the basestation, using RIS for auxiliary transmission, enabling the demodulation sequence of a user U1 to be prior to U2, and obtaining demodulation signal-to-noise ratios of the user ends U1 and U2; defining a security rate of the system; constructing a joint optimization equation; and dividing the joint optimization equation into two steps of beam forming and power distribution to be solved. A system comprises a power distribution module; a security rate definition module; a joint optimization equation construction module; and a joint optimization equation solving module. title: NATURAL GRAPHITE, MODIFIED NATURAL GRAPHITE MATERIAL PREPARED THEREFROM, PREPARATION METHOD, AND APPLICATIONS
assignee: BTR NEW MAT GROUP CO LTD [CN]
abstract: Provided are a natural graphite, a modified natural graphite material prepared therefrom, a preparation method, and applications. The natural graphite has the following characteristics: a particle size D50 smaller than 10µm, and D90/D10<2.5; a 002 interplanar spacing ranging from 0.336 to 0.360 nm, I002/I110≤35; a saturated tap density greater than or equal to 0.6g/cc and smaller than 1.3g/cc; and a specific surface area greater than or equal to 1.0m<2>/g and smaller than 10.0m<2>/g. The provided natural graphite has specific particle size, orientation, tap density and surface area and can serve as a raw material for preparing modified natural graphite material, and the prepared modified natural graphite material has high isotropy and thus has better rate performance and cycle performance.

title: Timer services
abstract: A request 6a to apply an update to a given function configured as replicas of the given function on a plurality of nodes 112 in a cluster of nodes providing replicas of functions is received from a client 110 at a first node 112a. The update request comprises an identifier for the given function. The given function identifier comprises information encoded using a probabilistic set such as a bloom filter or quotient filter. The information is associated with each of the nodes in the plurality of nodes on which the given function has been configured. The encoded information is decoded 6b to identify each of the nodes in the plurality of nodes on which respective replicas of the given function have been configured. At least one replica function update message 6c, 6e is transmitted to one or more of the identified nodes to instruct update 6d, 6f of their respective configured replicas of the given function according to the update requested by the client. title: CSI METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR CSI PARAMETER CONFIGURATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate after a 4G communication system, such as LTE. A method of operating user equipment (UE) of the present disclosure comprises the processes of: receiving CSI feedback configuration information; generating CSI feedback based on the CSI feedback configuration information; and transmitting the CSI feedback to a BS through an uplink channel.

title: CCN-based new node value and content popularity caching method in 6G
abstract: The invention discloses a CCN-based new node value and content popularity caching method in 6G, and the method comprises the steps that: content is cached in a CCN to support various functions, including content distribution and multicast; a node records the corresponding state and interface information in an interest packet request process, and a data packet is skipped back to a user according tothe information; through a content repository CS, a forwarding information base FIB and a pending interest table PIT are kept inside the CCN node; the popularity of the content is estimated through content request counting in the measurement process, so that the utilization rate of the cache space is further improved, and better cache performance is realized in the CCN; a variable is designed tomatch content popularity and a given node value, a proposed caching strategy is evaluated by an NDN-SIM simulator, and then simulation result analysis is carried out. abstract: The disclosure relates to a communication method and system for converging a 5th-Generation (5G) communication system or a 6th-Generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th-Generation (4G) system. title: DISPLAY DEVICE, DISPLAY DEVICE MANUFACTURING METHOD, DISPLAY DEVICE MANUFACTURING APPARATUS
abstract: A display device (1) comprises a shared hole transport layer (6C) shared by a plurality of subpixels (2R, 2G, 2B) between an anode (4) and light emitting layers (8R, 8G, 8B), and an individual hole transport layer (6P) is individually provided for each of the plurality of subpixels between the common hole transport layer and the light emitting layers. The shared hole transport layer is configuredusing a first hole transport layer material. A first individual hole transport layer (6B) included in a first-color sub-pixel (2B) is configured using a second hole transport layer material. Second individual hole transport layers (6R, 6G) included in the second-color subpixels (2R, 2G) is configured using a mixed material of the first hole transport layer material and the second hole transport layer material.

title: Antenna device covering Sub-6G frequency band
abstract: The utility model discloses an antenna structure for Sub6G, comprising a substrate, a PCB board, an antenna module, a feed line and a ground wire, the PCB board and the antenna module are respectivelyarranged on the substrate, the antenna module is connected with the feed port on the PCB board through the feed line, and the ground wire is connected with the feed port on the PCB board through thefeed line. title: APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR TRANSMITTING AND RECEIVING ASYNCHRONOUS SIGNAL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a communication method and system for converging a 5th-generation (5G) communication system or a 6th-generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th-generation (4G) system. The disclosure relates to an operating method of a terminal in a wireless communication system. The method may include: receiving configuration information about a plurality of signatures for non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) from a base station, the plurality of signatures including first signatures and second signatures; identifying a first signature sequence for the terminal, based on the configuration information; and transmitting data, configured based on the identified first signature sequence, to the base station, wherein the first signature sequence may be configured by repeating the first signatures, and the first signatures may be respectively applied to consecutive symbols of the data.

title: Double-frequency common-caliber phased-array antenna device suitable for Sub 6G
abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of antennas, and discloses a double-frequency common-caliber phased-array antenna device suitable for Sub 6G, which comprises a first antenna array plane and a second antenna array plane, and the first antenna array surface comprises a plurality of low-frequency antenna units which are uniformly arranged according to a rectangular grid or a triangular grid; the second antenna array surface comprises a plurality of high-frequency antenna units which are uniformly arranged according to a rectangular grid or a triangular grid; and the first antenna array surface and the second antenna array surface are combined in a staggered arrangement mode or a nested mode. According to the embodiment of the invention, firstly, two frequency bands are realized by using two different antenna units; secondly, an array plane is formed according to respective proper array spacing so as to ensure wide-angle scanning performance of two frequency bands; and finally

title: Cloud memory sharing method and system
assignee: FUJIAN TIANQUAN EDUCATION TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a cloud memory sharing method and system. According to the invention, the method comprises the steps that a cloud server pre-loads preset resources into a cloud memory; the client sends a resource loading request to the cloud server; the cloud server receives the resource loading request sent by the client, and synchronizes the preset resources preloaded in the memory to the client; the client receives the preset resources synchronized by the cloud server and writes the preset resources into a local memory. Based on the transmission speed of 5G and 6G high-speed networks, the speed of loading the cloud resources into the memory is higher than the speed of reading the resources from the disk, the resource data is preloaded through the cloud server, the speed of loading the resources into the memory is increased, and meanwhile, a large number of resources do not need to be loaded locally, so that the resource loading efficiency is improved. title: Lead frame preparation method based on etching process
abstract: The invention relates to preparation of a lead frame of an integrated circuit, in particular to a lead frame preparation method based on an etching process, and aims to solve the technical problem ofoverhigh production cost due to the fact that photoresist can only be used once in the processing process of an existing etching method. The preparation method comprises the following steps: processing a metal sheet or a polymer film material into a mask plate of a lead frame pattern, and coating one surface of the mask plate with viscous polymer materials such as PDMS; tightly attaching the face,provided with the viscous polymer material, of the mask plate to a copper base material to be etched and pressed in vacuum; placing the substrate in a FeCl3 solution with the temperature of 40-100 DEG C and the concentration of 0.1 g/ml-0. 6g/ml for etching; and taking down the etched copper base material from the mask plate to obtain the required lead frame. title: Samarium-iron-cobalt film electroplating liquid and preparation method thereof
abstract: The invention discloses a samarium-iron-cobalt film electroplating solution. The samarium-iron-cobalt film electroplating solution comprises the following components of, in mass concentration, 5-15g/Lof cobalt salt, 10-40g/L of ferric salt, 8-30g/L of samarium salt, 10-35g/L of complexing agent, 3-7g/L of boric acid, 2-5g/L of glacial acetic acid, 3-6g/L of sodium citrate, 1-3g/L of potassium phosphate or potassium nitrate and a solvent water, and the invention further discloses a preparation method of the samarium-iron-cobalt film electroplating solution. By adopting the electroplating solution disclosed by the invention, three elements of Sm, Fe and Co can be simultaneously deposited, and a samarium-iron-cobalt film prepared by adopting the electroplating solution has the advantages ofcontrollable element content, uniform element distribution and the like, the invention provides an excellent ligand and electroplating liquid for development of a new generation of rare earth permanent

title: Mainboard assembly with antenna and capable of realizing sub-6G and communication equipment
abstract: The utility model discloses a mainboard assembly with an antenna capable of realizing sub6G and communication equipment, the mainboard assembly comprises a mainboard and a patch antenna arranged on the mainboard, and the mainboard comprises a substrate and a mainboard ground extension structure connected to one end of the substrate; the substrate comprises a clearance area opposite to the mainboard ground extension structure, and the patch antenna is attached in the clearance area; the mainboard ground extension structure is coupled with the clearance area and the patch antenna to achieve a sub 6G frequency band, the mainboard assembly is provided with the mainboard ground extension structure connected with the substrate, N41, N77, N78 and N79 frequency band transmission is met through coupling of the mainboard ground extension structure, the clearance area and the patch antenna, in addition, the sub 6G requirement for high-speed signal transmission is met, and the cost is lowered. title: FACILITATING SPARSITY ADAPTIVE FEEDBACK IN THE DELAY DOPPLER DOMAIN IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating sparsity adaptive feedback in the delay doppler domain in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise determining, by a first device comprising a processor, a channel covariance matrix in a time-frequency domain based on a channel estimation associated with reference signals received from a second device. The method also can comprise decomposing, by the first device, the channel covariance matrix into a group of component matrices. Further, the method can comprise transforming, by the first device, respective matrices of the group of component matrices into respective covariance matrices in a delay doppler domain. The method also can comprise determining, by the first device, channel state information feedback in the delay doppler domain.

title: PERFORMANCE BASED ON INFERRED USER EQUIPMENT DEVICE SPEED FOR ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating improved performance based on inferred user equipment device speed for advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise estimating a speed of a user equipment device based on a number of times that a layer indicator associated with the user equipment device changes during a defined period of time. The operations can also comprise selecting a multiple input transmission mode for a transmission to the user equipment device based on the speed of the user equipment device, resulting in a selected transmission mode. A closed loop multiple input transmission mode can be selected in response to the speed being below a defined speed. Alternatively, an open loop multiple input transmission mode can be selected in response to the speed being above the defined speed.

title: AGGREGATION OF APPLICATION AND SYSTEMS FOR SERVICES FOR 6G OR OTHER NEXT GENERATION NETWORK
abstract: In a 6G network, microservices can be utilized in the absence of a core network. For example, after a mobile device has authenticated, when the mobile device moves away from mobile edge computing (MEC) coverage, the information from the MEC can be ported to a new MEC along the way of the mobile device's path. The initial MEC can send service related application package data to one or more neighboring MECs. When a neighboring MEC of the neighboring MECs receives the service related application package data and the neighboring MEC serves the area which the mobile device is transitioning to, then the neighboring MEC can send an acknowledgment back to the initial MEC. Once the acknowledgment is received by the initial MEC, the initial MEC can complete the handover of the application data needed to reduce or mitigate any disruption in service for the mobile device.

title: Broadband 5G antenna
assignee: CONCENTRA PRECISION TECH JIANGSU CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a broadband 5G antenna. The antenna comprises a substrate, a first radiation oscillator and a second radiation oscillator; both the first radiation oscillator and the second radiation oscillator are of a symmetrical structure taking the axis of the substrate as the center line; a central feeder line is formed in the direction, facing the second radiation oscillator, of thefirst radiation oscillator along the central line, and a feeding point is arranged at the tail end of the central feeder line; the second radiation oscillator is provided with a grounding point on thecentral line and is provided with a concave cavity capable of accommodating the central feeder line; and in an area where the central feeder line is matched with the concave cavity, at least one section of area of the central feeder line and/or the concave cavity is different from other areas in width. The broadband 5G antenna is wide in bandwidth, not only meets the domestic 5G high-frequency requ

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR UCI MULTIPLEXING IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). The methods and apparatus in a wireless communication system comprises: determining a number of bits for a PUSCH; generating a first set of bits for CG-UCI; generating a second set of bits for a HARQ feedback; multiplexing first set of bits for CG-UCI and second set of bits for HARQ feedback into PUSCH, if a sum of first and second set of bits does not exceed determined number of bits for PUSCH, wherein first set of bits for CG-UCI is multiplexed with a higher priority than second set of bits for HARQ feedback; and transmitting, to a BS over an uplink channel, PUSCH including multiplexed first and second set of bits for CG-UCI and HARQ feedback, respectively.

title: FACILITATING MOBILITY AS A SERVICE IN FIFTH GENERATION (5G) OR OTHER ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating mobility as a service in advanced networks (e.g., 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise evaluating, by a system comprising a processor, a group of characteristics of a device. The method also can comprise enabling, by the system, a mobility management function as a mobility microservice. Further, the method can comprise activating, by the system, microservices of a group of microservices, comprising the mobility microservice, based on the group of characteristics of the device.

title: Dual-frequency dual-polarized micro base station antenna suitable for 5G full band and dual-antenna system
abstract: The invention discloses a dual-frequency dual-polarized micro base station antenna suitable for a 5G full band below 6G Hz and a dual-antenna system, which are suitable for 5G mobile communication. The dual-frequency dual-polarized micro base station antenna comprises a substrate metal backboard, a dielectric substrate suspended and erected on the metal backboard, two pairs of metal eccentric circular rings printed on the bottom surface of the dielectric substrate and placed along diagonal lines of +/-45 degrees, four small circular metal patches located in the circular rings, two pairs of feed coaxial lines, a Y-shaped coupling feed metal strip, metal columns which are used for respectively connecting the metal backboard with the two eccentric circular rings positioned at the upper part,and four metal columns which are used for connecting the metal backboard with the small circular patches. By adopting a standard double-sided printed circuit process and a simple metal column structure

title: Wide-frequency-band 6G four-input four-output bridge structure
abstract: The utility model discloses a broadband 6G four-in four-out bridge structure, comprising an alloy cavity, the front end and the rear end of the alloy cavity are respectively screwed with a group of radio frequency connectors through screw threads, each group comprises four radio frequency connectors, two groups of radio frequency connectors are symmetrically distributed front and back, a snakelikestrip-shaped transmission line is arranged in the alloy cavity, and the snakelike strip-shaped transmission line is arranged in the alloy cavity. The end of the snakelike strip-shaped transmission line is fixedly connected with a center needle of the radio frequency connector, the snakelike strip-shaped transmission line is fixedly connected with a Teflon column, the upper portion and the lower portion of the alloy cavity are each connected with an alloy inner cover in an inserted mode, and the upper end and the lower end of the alloy cavity are each fixedly connected with an alloy outer cover

title: Method of data-efficient threat detection in a computer network
abstract: In a computer network 1, wherein the network may be formed of interconnected nodes 5a-5g representing EDR (Endpoint Detection & Response) endpoints onto which data collectors/sensors 6a-6g are installed, a data-efficient threat detection method comprises: receiving raw data related to a network node, generating local behaviour models related to the node, wherein dissimilar data types are aligned as input events; generating at least one common model of normal behaviour on the basis of local behaviour models related to multiple nodes; filtering events by estimating the likelihood that they are produced by the common model and/or by the one or more local behaviour models, wherein only input events having a likelihood below a predetermined threshold are passed through the filtering; and processing events passed through the filtering for generating a security related decision. Further action may then be taken, such as activating a node firewall or quarantining. A node may generate its o

title: CELLULAR SYSTEM
abstract: A system includes one or more antennas and a processor to communicate with a predetermined target using 5G or 6G protocols.

title: FAST MULTI-BEAM LISTEN BEFORE TALK
abstract: Facilitating fast multi-beam listen before talk in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a device can comprise determining a first inactivity in a first beam based on a first performance of a first listen before talk procedure in the first beam and, based on determining the first inactivity, analyzing a presence of an activity in a second beam based on a second performance of a second listen before talk procedure in the second beam. Further, the operations can comprise, based on determining a lack of the presence of the activity in the second beam, transmitting a signal via the second beam. In an example, analyzing the presence of the activity in the second beam can be performed prior to completion of the determining the first inactivity in the first beam.

title: METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR PROVIDING PAGING CAUSE TO MUSIM USER EQUIPMENT
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). Embodiments herein provide a method of an Access and Mobility management Function (AMF) controller in a wireless communication network. The method includes receiving, from a Multi Universal SIM User Equipment (MUSIM UE), a NAS request message including an AI request indicating a request for a paging cause in a paging message. Further, the method includes storing the AI request received in the NAS request message. Further, the method includes providing a NAS response message including an AI to the MUSIM UE, wherein the AI indicates whether the paging cause will be provided to the MUSIM UE as part of one of a paging procedure and a NAS notification procedure.

title: VIRTUAL TRACKING OR REGISTRATION AREAS FOR NON TERRESTRIAL NETWORKS
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a communication method and system for converging a 5th-Generation (5G) communication system or a 6th-Generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th-Generation (4G) system. A method and apparatus in a wireless communication system is provided. The method and apparatus comprises: identifying a virtual tracking area (VTA) to tracking area (TA) (VTA-TA) mapping information indicating mappings between (i) TA identifiers (TAIs) associated with a non-terrestrial network (NTN) entity and (ii) VTAs associated with geographical areas as a function of time; identifying one or more TAIs at an instant or during a time period based on the VTA-TA mapping information; transmitting a radio resource control (RRC) request message including the one or more TAIs; and receiving an RRC response message corresponding to the RRC request message based on the one or more TAIs. The RRC response message includes a virtual registration area (VRA)

title: CAPABILITY COORDINATION FOR MOBILITY WITH DAPS
abstract: A 5th generation (5G) communication system or a 6th generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th generation (4G) communication system, such as long-term evolution (LTE) are provided. A method of dual active protocol stack (DAPS), handover of a user equipment (UE), from a source gNB to a target gNB is described. The method includes receiving, from the UE, UE capability information, including DAPS capabilities, coordinating, by the source gNB, a DAPS handover request for the UE, based, at least in part, on the DAPS capabilities of the UE, and reconfiguring, by the source gNB, the UE from the source gNB to the target gNB.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR MULTIPLEXING PARTIAL CSI
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). A method for operating a UE for CSI reporting comprises receiving configuration information for a CSI report, where the configuration information configures a PMI codebook and partitions the PMI codebook into first and second PMI subsets respectively indicating first and second subsets of a set of components S; determining, based on the configuration information, the CSI report comprising first and second CSI parts, the second CSI part including the second PMI subset and partitioned into one or more groups; selecting at least one group from the one or more groups of the second CSI part based on a resource allocation for transmission of uplink control information (UCI), and transmitting, through the transceiver, over an uplink (UL) channel, the UCI including a first UCI part and a second UCI part, th

title: CAPABILITY COORDINATION FOR MOBILITY WITH DAPS
abstract: A 5th generation (5G) communication system or a 6th generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th generation (4G) communication system, such as long-term evolution (LTE) are provided. A method of dual active protocol stack (DAPS), handover of a user equipment (UE), from a source gNB to a target gNB is described. The method includes receiving, from the UE, UE capability information, including DAPS capabilities, coordinating, by the source gNB, a DAPS handover request for the UE, based, at least in part, on the DAPS capabilities of the UE, and reconfiguring, by the source gNB, the UE from the source gNB to the target gNB.

title: FACILITATING A TIME-DIVISION MULTIPLEXING PATTERN-BASED SOLUTION FOR A DUAL-SUBSCRIBER IDENTITY MODULE WITH SINGLE RADIO IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating a time-division multiplexing pattern-based solution for a dual-subscriber identity module with a single radio in advanced networks (e.g., 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise obtaining, by a network device comprising a processor, multiple time-division multiplexing patterns applicable to a mobile device. The multiple time-division multiplexing patterns indicate respective repeating patterns of an on state and an off state of a defined identity of the mobile device. The method also can comprise facilitating, by the network device, a transmission, to the mobile device, of information indicative of the multiple time-division multiplexing patterns. In an example, the mobile device can be configured to operate with at least two subscriber identity modules.

title: Intention-driven 6G network intelligent operation and maintenance method, system, device and application
abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of wireless communication, and discloses an intention-driven 6G network intelligent operation and maintenance method, system and application, and the method comprises the steps: translating an original operator intention represented by a natural language into an intention standard expression which can be recognized by a network through an intention translation module; realizing intelligent recombination of a fine-grained strategy through an intention intelligent strategy generation module, and generating an intelligent new strategy meeting the intention; and introducing a deep neural evolutionary network, performing feature extraction on a collected fault information set to obtain the type, position, number, fault source information and fault occurrence time of the fault, constructing a network fault topological graph, and performing fault positioning and repairing. Through introduction of the AI, network operation and maintenance are transf

title: Facilitating adaptive power spectral density with chromatic spectrum optimization in fifth generation (5G) or other advanced networks
abstract: Facilitating adaptive power spectral density with chromatic spectrum optimization in advanced networks (e.g., 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise evaluating, by a system comprising a processor, a capture rate of mobile devices within a radio access network. The capture rate is representative of a quantity of mobile devices using a millimeter wave spectrum of the radio access network. The method also can comprise facilitating, by the system, an adjustment to a power spectral density of the radio access network based on a determination that the capture rate fails to satisfy a target capture rate of mobile devices using the millimeter wave spectrum.

title: Sub-6G antenna with strong applicability
assignee: DONGGUAN SEYA ELECTRONIC COMMUNICATION CO LTD
abstract: The utility model discloses a Sub6G antenna with strong applicability, which belongs to the technical field of antennas and comprises a shell, a first mounting hole is dug in the left end of the shell, two sliding blocks are connected in the first mounting hole in a sliding manner, sliding grooves are dug in the upper end and the lower end of the first mounting hole, and sliding rods are fixedly connected to the upper ends and the lower ends of the two sliding blocks. The four sliding rods are connected into the two sliding grooves in a sliding mode respectively, first inserting grooves are formed in the ends, away from each other, of the four sliding rods, two second installing holes are formed in the upper end and the lower end of the shell respectively, first installing grooves are formed in the circumferential inner walls of the four second installing holes respectively, and inserting rods are movably inserted into the four second installing holes respectively. The four inserting r

title: Radio frequency signal source capable of being calibrated in real time
abstract: The invention discloses a radio frequency signal source capable of being calibrated in real time. The radio frequency signal source is composed of a reference source circuit, a phase-locked loop circuit, a filter circuit and a control circuit. The reference source circuit is used for providing a reference signal for the phase-locked loop circuit; the phase-locked loop circuit adopts a broadband frequency synthesizer integrated with a VCO to form a closed loop to generate a frequency signal. The filter circuit realizes segmented filtering of the frequency signal through branching, and outputs a 20M-6G signal; and the control circuit performs gain control and real-time calibration on the 20M-6G signals output by the filter circuit, and outputs a required radio frequency signal source. According to the signal source, the broadband frequency synthesizer integrated with the VCO is adopted to replace a traditional VCO, the cost is reduced, and meanwhile stray is optimized.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSCEIVING SIGNAL USING ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). A method of transmitting or receiving a signal by a user equipment (UE) in a mobile communication system is provided. The method may include: identifying a neural network model for transmitting first information to a base station (BS); learning a connection weight of the neural network model using the first information; transmitting, to the base station, second information for updating a weight of a second partial neural network corresponding to the base station based on a result of the learning; and updating a weight of a first partial neural network corresponding to the UE based on the result of the learning.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSCEIVING SIGNAL USING ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). A method of transmitting or receiving a signal by a user equipment (UE) in a mobile communication system is provided. The method may include: identifying a neural network model for transmitting first information to a base station (BS); learning a connection weight of the neural network model using the first information; transmitting, to the base station, second information for updating a weight of a second partial neural network corresponding to the base station based on a result of the learning; and updating a weight of a first partial neural network corresponding to the UE based on the result of the learning.

title: METHOD AND UE FOR HANDLING RESOURCE ALLOCATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). Accordingly, embodiments herein disclose a system and method for handling resource allocation in wireless communication system. The method includes registering, by a UE, a first SIM among a plurality of SIMs with a first network entity in the wireless communication system and a second SIM among plurality of SIMs with a second network entity in wireless communication system. Further, the method includes configuring, by the UE, a RF resource with the first SIM for at least one of a downlink reception and uplink transmission. Further, the method includes determining, by the UE, whether second SIM needs RF resource. Further, the method includes performing, by the UE, provisioning of RF resource based on the determination.

title: SENSOR SYSTEM AND SENSOR UNIT
abstract: A sensor system (100) to be mounted in a vehicle (1) is provided with: a LiDAR (6f); a camera (6g); a housing (121) accommodating the LiDAR (6f) and the camera (6g); and a cleaner (103) having at least one nozzle (131) that is attached to the housing (121) and is capable of cleaning simultaneously a first surface to be cleaned (123a), which corresponds to the LiDAR (6f), and a second surface to be cleaned (123b), which corresponds to the camera (6g), with a cleaning medium.

title: SIGNAL TRANSMISSION METHOD AND DEVICE FOR UE
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). Provided is a signal transmission method and device for User Equipment (UE). The signal transmission method for UE includes: receiving information for determining an uplink/downlink attribute of each frequency domain resource in each slot/symbol transmitted by the base station; determining the uplink/downlink attribute of each time-frequency resource according to the information for determining the uplink/downlink attribute of each frequency domain resource in each slot/symbol; and performing signal transmission according to the determined uplink/downlink attribute of each time-frequency resource.

title: Single-port high-order OAM radiator based on discrete periodic unit SSPP mode
abstract: The invention discloses a single-port high-order OAM radiator based on a discrete periodic unit SSPP mode, and relates to the field of antennas. The radiator comprises a high-frequency dielectric plate and a metal patch. The metal patch part is sequentially divided into a coplanar waveguide, a coplanar wave guide periodic SSPP waveguide transition part, a periodic SSPP waveguide, a periodic SSPP wave guide to radiation ring transition part and a radiation ring; the coplanar waveguide, the coplanar wave guide periodic SSPP waveguide transition part and the periodic SSPP waveguide jointly form an SSPP excitation and transmission structure; the periodic SSPP wave guide to radiation ring transition part and the radiation ring jointly form an annular OAM radiator, a radiation source directly comes from a discrete periodic unit, and vortex radiation is directly generated by utilizing SSPP. The whole patch structure is miniaturized and is easy to integrate. And 11-order OAM and 12-order OAM c

title: Three-in-one digital intelligent traffic vehicle-mounted terminal based on Beidou or GPS navigation
assignee: NANJING SIMONS ENTERPRISE MAN CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides a three-certificate-in-one digital intelligent traffic vehicle-mounted terminal based on Beidou or GPS navigation. The terminal comprises a Beidou satellite positioning system or a GPS positioning system, wherein vehicle-mounted terminals and vehicles are arranged in a one-to-one correspondence manner, the vehicle-mounted terminals are provided with vehicle-mounted Beidou antennas, and the vehicle-mounted Beidou antennas are connected with the Beidou satellite positioning system or the GPS positioning system; two-way data transmission is adopted between the vehicle-mounted Beidou antenna and the Beidou satellite positioning system or the GPS positioning system, a power supply module is arranged in the vehicle-mounted terminal, the power supply module supplies power to the vehicle-mounted terminal and the external vehicle-mounted Beidou antenna, and the vehicle-mounted terminal comprises a display module arranged on the outer side of the vehicle-mounted terminal.

title: 6G radome suitable for terahertz antenna and preparation method thereof
assignee: AEROSPACE RES INST SPECIAL MATERIAL & PROCESS TECHNOLOGY
abstract: The invention relates to a 6G radome suitable for a terahertz antenna and a preparation method thereof. The radome comprises an outer skin, a foam core layer, a middle skin, a foam core layer and an inner skin. The 6G radome is characterized in that the prepreg formed by combination of fiber reinforcement and resin is used as the skin material. As for the prpreg, the resin is polyphenylene ether modified bisphenol M cyanate ester resin and has the following dielectric properties: dielectric constant is less than or equal to 2. 6 and the loss factor is less than or equal to 0. 004. The fiber reinforcement is quartz fiber braid. The prepreg gum content is 30wt%-44wt%. The hot-pressing pot forming technology is used. The radome has high wave transmittance within the range of 100-200GHz and can meet the transmission performance requirements of the 6G antenna network within the terahertz band.

title: All-phase dynamic modulator
assignee: SHENZHEN INST OF ADV TECH CAS
abstract: The invention provides an all-phase dynamic modulator, dynamic modulation is carried out on a phase in a 2pi range by utilizing Fermi level change characteristics of graphene, the problem that in the prior art, a graphene reflection metasurface is difficult to achieve 0-360-degree all-phase dynamic continuous regulation and control in a terahertz wave band can be solved, and the all-phase dynamic modulator also has the advantage of relatively high reflection efficiency; under the working frequency, the reflection efficiency reaches more than 20%. In addition, the graphene-metal structure designed by the invention is novel and simple, and has feasibility in experiment. The all-phase dynamic modulator provided by the invention has wide potential application in the fields of radars, safety systems, biological imaging, future sixth-generation wireless network communication and the like.

title: UE SIGNAL TRANSMISSION METHOD AND DEVICE FOR UE
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a data transmission rate higher than a 4G communication system such as LTE. Provided are a signal transmission method for user equipment (UE) and a device. The signal transmission method for the UE includes the following steps of: receiving information for determining uplink/downlink attributes of each frequency domain resource from each slot/symbol transmitted by a base station; determining uplink/downlink attributes of each time-frequency resource in accordance with the information for determining uplink/downlink attributes of each frequency domain resource from each slot/symbol; and performing signal transmission in accordance with the determined uplink/downlink attributes of each time-frequency resource.

title: User-centered unmanned aerial vehicle group construction method based on Backhaul capacity perception
abstract: Due to high dynamic mobility and a large probability line-of-sight link of an unmanned aerial vehicle base station, unmanned aerial vehicle communication becomes an important solution of a B5G/6G network, however, a non-ideal Backhaul link of the unmanned aerial vehicle base station limits transmission of user data and becomes a bottleneck of network capacity improvement. The embodiment of the invention provides a user-centered unmanned aerial vehicle group construction method based on Backhaul capacity perception. The method includes: dynamically constructing an unmanned aerial vehicle groupfor the user based on the capacity of the unmanned aerial vehicle Backhaul link and the BRSRP value calculated by the reference signal receiving power, so as to enable the user to always feel that theuser is in the center of the cell. Particularly, N unmanned aerial vehicles with the maximum BRSRP value in the network form an unmanned aerial vehicle group of the user, and the unmanned aerial vehicl

title: Quartering microstrip power divider
abstract: The invention discloses a quartering microstrip power divider. The quartering microstrip power divider comprises a shell (1), a signal input and output port group (2) and a power divider assembly (3), wherein the power divider assembly (3) adopts a multi-branch snakelike structure to output power distribution signals and has a plurality of power dividers in topological connection. On the basis of Wilkinson power divider topology, five-branch snakelike routing is adopted to broaden the frequency band, so that the frequency band is expanded to DC-6G; the length of each branch is reduced to be smaller than lambda/4, and the coupling distance of each branch is shortened as far as possible to reduce the size; through measures of adjusting characteristic impedance of each branch and the like, microstrip isolation is improved, the size and performance of the coupler are enabled to be optimal, and the requirement of miniaturization design and the requirement of isolation are met. The invention

title: Low energy method of making a cathode composition
abstract: A low energy milling method of making a cathode composition for a lithium ion battery of general formula: Li1+Mn1−O2 The method comprises the step of providing at least one lithium manganese oxide precursor and milling the at least one precursor for a period of time. The milling speed can be 400rpm or lower and/or the milling media may have a density of 6g/cm-3 or less. Additives and binders may also be added such as carbon black.

title: LIGHT EMITTING ELEMENT AND LIGHT EMITTING DEVICE
abstract: Each of light emitting elements (6R, 6G, 6B) according to the present invention is provided at least with a positive electrode (8), a negative electrode (20), a light emitting layer (14) that is arranged between the positive electrode and the negative electrode, and a hole transport layer (10) that is arranged between the positive electrode and the light emitting layer. The hole transport layer is a mixture film that contains a hole transport material and a polysiloxane polymer. Each of the light emitting elements is additionally provided with: a first electron transport layer (16) that is arranged between the light emitting layer and the negative electrode; and a second electron transport layer (18) that is arranged between the first electron transport layer and the negative electrode. The value of the LUMO level of the first electron transport layer is not less than the value of the LUMO level of the second electron transport layer; and the value of the LUMO level of the first electr

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR HANDING OVER TERMINAL IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM USING ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system supporting higher data transmission rates than 4G systems such as LTE systems. The present invention of the present disclosure pertains to a method for handing over a terminal in a wireless communication system, wherein the method is characterized by: receiving handover control reference information from a base station; determining whether to perform a handover on the basis of the handover control reference information and wireless signal information acquired through the measurement of a wireless signal; and transmitting a handover request message to the base station when it is determined that handover is to be performed.

title: FLEXIBLE HIGH CAPACITY-RADIO NETWORK TEMPORARY IDENTIFIER
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). The present disclosure provides methods and apparatuses in a wireless communication system. A method of operating a base station (BS) includes generating an indicator indicating that the BS is capable of supporting a high-capacity radio network temporary identifier (HC-RNTI), wherein a size of the HC-RNTI is based on a configurable granularity; generating a system information block including the indicator and information indicating the size of the HC-RNTI; transmitting, to a user equipment (UE), the system information block; and transmitting, to the UE, the HC-RNTI, wherein the HC-RNTI is allocated to the UE.

title: FACILITATING IMPROVED PERFORMANCE OF MULTIPLE DOWNLINK CONTROL CHANNELS IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating improved performance of multiple downlink control channels in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise scheduling multiple data traffic channels for a user equipment device. Scheduling the multiple data channels can comprise scheduling a first data traffic channel of the multiple data traffic channels based on a legacy scheduling procedure and scheduling a second data traffic channel of the multiple data traffic channels based on an iterative procedure. The operations can also comprise transmitting, to the user equipment device, first information via multiple downlink control channels and transmitting, to the user equipment device, second information via the multiple data traffic channels.

title: Deployment parameter setting method considering distance constraint between unmanned aerial vehicles
abstract: The unmanned aerial vehicle base station becomes an important research direction of 6G in the future by virtue of large-probability sight distance link and rapid deployment capability of the unmanned aerial vehicle base station. However, in consideration of the flight safety of the unmanned aerial vehicle and reduction of the generation probability of an LoS mutual interference link, the position deployment of the unmanned aerial vehicle has a repulsion relationship, that is, distance constraint exists between individuals. The embodiment of the invention provides a deployment parameter setting method considering distance constraints between unmanned aerial vehicles, and the deployment number and deployment positions of the unmanned aerial vehicles are adjusted based on a set exclusion distance D. Particularly, according to initial deployment position parameters of the unmanned aerial vehicles, the unmanned aerial vehicles are divided into different sets, meanwhile, a weight factor mu (

title: FACILITATING SELECTION OF DEMODULATION REFERENCE SIGNAL PORTS IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating selection of demodulation reference signal port combinations in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise evaluating a capability of a mobile device. The operations can also comprise assigning a first group of port combinations for the mobile device based on the capability of the mobile device being a first capability and a second group of port combinations for the mobile device based on the capability of the mobile device being a second capability, resulting in a port combination assignment. The port combination assignment can mitigate a peak-to-average power ratio value.

title: Sub-6G low-frequency band-based millimeter wave antenna
assignee: KUNSHAN RUIXIANG XUNTONG COMM TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides a sub-6G low-frequency band-based millimeter wave antenna. The sub-6G low-frequency band-based millimeter wave antenna comprises a substrate and a first antenna structure, the first antenna structure comprises a first signal strip, a first strip and a second strip which are located on the same layer of the substrate, and the first strip and the second strip are symmetrically distributed on the two sides of the first signal strip in the length direction of the first signal strip. Thus, stray capacitance effects are generated between the first strip and the first signal strip and between the second strip and the first signal strip, which are equivalent to that a capacitor is connected in parallel and an inductor is connected in series between the signal strip and the strip. Therefore, when the low-frequency-band current and the millimeter-wave current flow through the first signal band at the same time, the millimeter-wave frequency band is conducted and the low-fr

title: Novel material layer structure
abstract: The invention discloses a novel material layer structure which comprises an upper curing high-frequency material layer, an upper thin film arranged on the upper surface of the upper curing high-frequency material layer and a second upper line layer arranged on the upper surface of the upper thin film. The novel material layer structure provided by the application not only can greatly simplify the layer structure and reduce the overall thickness, but also has the function of high-speed transmission of high-frequency signals, and is especially suitable for novel 5G and 6G science and technology products; and the protection and resistance effects are achieved on the copper ion migration phenomenon when the lines are electrified, and safe and normal work of the lines is guaranteed.

title: A METHOD FOR IOT ENABLED ENERGY EFFICIENT 6G COMMUNICATION
abstract: A METHOD FOR IOT ENABLED ENERGY EFFICIENT 6G COMMUNICATION Aspects of the present disclosure relate to the method (100) for IoT enabled 6G communication . 6G signals are high frequency signals beyond 60 GHz which enables the 5 availability of higher bandwidth availability and greater speed. In this method (100) a transceiver (2) is used to transmit the signal through the shaft (1) to each space in the area.
